contest.title: Codeforces Round 1092 (Unrated, Div. 1, Based on THUPC 2026 Finals)

sources.notes: Statement and constraints: count base/block-length representations with equal digits inside every block.
Luogu mirror used to cross-check statement and constraints.
Used to confirm the p=2 divisor enumeration and p>=3 geometric-sum bound.

reference_candidates.notes: Candidate Luogu solution page; not used as evidence in this review.

annotation.summary: Count ordered pairs (b,p), b,p>=2, such that the base-b representation of n can be partitioned into length-p blocks whose digits are equal inside each block.
annotation.constraints: n <= 1e12 and t <= 1000; O(sqrt n) divisor/base enumeration is intended.
annotation.core_idea: For p=2, every equal digit pair contributes a multiple of b+1, so b+1 must divide n; enumerate divisors and verify digits. For p>=3, the repunit 1+b+...+b^{p-1} must divide n, which bounds b by n^(1/(p-1)); enumerate feasible p and small b, then verify digit blocks directly.
annotation.complexity: O(sqrt n) dominant time per test in the editorial approach.
tricks: verify digit grouping after divisibility filtering

solution_variants.name: divisor-and-small-base-enumeration
solution_variants.summary: Enumerate divisors d=b+1 of n for p=2, enumerate p>=3 with bases bounded by the repunit sum, and verify each candidate by scanning base-b digits in blocks.

tags.evidence: The main pruning comes from necessary divisibility by b+1 for p=2 and by 1+b+...+b^{p-1} for p>=3.
After deriving tight bounds, the solution enumerates candidate divisors, bases, and block lengths and verifies them.
Candidate verification repeatedly takes n mod b to read base-b digits.
